name: agent-skills-marketplace
description: A curated collection of Claude Code skills and plugins for developers

plugins.name: daily-carry
plugins.description: Essential daily-use skills: Portainer deployment management and Tech Research Enforcer for verified coding answers

plugins.name: release-automation
plugins.description: Automated release workflow for any project type with version bumping, changelog generation, documentation sync, and git operations. Supports Node.js, Python, Rust, Go, Java, generic projects, monorepos, and Claude Code plugins.

plugins.name: diataxis
plugins.description: Documentation writing and review skills based on the Diátaxis framework. Seven skills covering all four documentation quadrants (tutorial, how-to, reference, explanation), plus README landing pages, a doc planner, and a docs reviewer.

plugins.name: sdlc-workflow
plugins.description: Complete SDLC workflow as four skill-mode routers plus the wf-design router and a setup-wide-logging command. /wf executes the canonical 13-stage lifecycle (shape, slice, plan, implement, verify, review, handoff, ship, retro) plus four perf/observability augmentations. /wf-quick (10 sub-commands) attaches output to an existing workflow as a compressed slice when the first positional arg matches a slug in `.ai/workflows/INDEX.md`; otherwise it runs standalone — except `probe`, which is slug-mode only. v9.14.0 closes the 'verified but actually broken' gap with three coordinated changes: (1) `/wf verify` gains a user-observable AC gate that refuses `result: pass` when runtime evidence is missing — new `result: blocked-runtime-evidence-missing` variant distinguishes procedural fail from substantive fail; partition uses a hybrid wording heuristic with optional `observable:` AC-level override. (2) New `/wf-quick probe <slug> [target]` for runtime-truth verification — drives the running artifact, captures observable output, four-layer target resolution (AC text / slice / surface inference / ad-hoc), focus/filter scope (default focus reports incidentals; `--strict` archives them to a side file), multi-target via `--from <path>`, `--adapter <key>` narrowing (default run-all-matched), branch-posture AskUserQuestion on mismatch, two-phase bootstrap (try-to-resolve then write `awaiting-environment` slice with remediation hint). (3) Shared runtime-adapter registry (`skills/wf/reference/runtime-adapters.md`) keyed by platform (web/android/ios/cli/desktop/service/notebook) consumed by both verify and probe. Deferral escape hatch (`interactive-verification: deferred`) recorded under `00-index.md.runtime-evidence-deferrals[]`; review/handoff warn but proceed, ship HARD-BLOCKS. `/wf-meta status` dashboards gain a `Runtime` column (`clean`/`deferrals: N`/`probe-findings: N`). v9.13.0 repurposed `/wf-quick discover` and `/wf-quick investigate` from product-strategy gates to engineering hypothesis-test (FOR/AGAINST/counter-hypothesis) and solution-options sketcher (2–3 distinct approaches, no winner). v9.12.0 rewrites `/wf-meta init-ship-plan` from template-instantiation to **discovery → hypothesis → confirm**: Step 1 reads CI workflow YAML, infra-as-code, package manifests, release tooling, and runbook directories before any AskUserQuestion; Step 2 walks each required-core block (A–G) as a hypothesis derived from discovery with `Other (describe)` always present; Step 3 adds open-schema `additional-contracts[]` extensions (data-migration, feature-flag-rollout, infrastructure-as-code, mobile-app-store, compliance-gate, data-pipeline, schema-registry) for project-specific shape; templates demoted from control-flow branches to hypothesis seeds. `/wf ship` reads the required core unchanged, so no migration. v9.11.0 extended INDEX.md to four more read paths: `/wf-meta status/resume/next` enumerate from the registry, `/wf intake` checks the registry before creating and prompts on duplicate slugs (closed slugs cannot be reused), and `/wf` gained a centralized Step 0.5 fuzzy-suggest for typo'd slugs across the 13 slug-consuming sub-commands. `/wf-meta sync` owns the registry — bootstrap on first run, refresh on every run. /wf-design dispatches across 22 design sub-commands. /review runs per-dimension or named-sweep code review across 31 dimensions and 7 sweeps via parallel sub-agent dispatch. wf-validate pre-write hook gates artifact writes and warns (non-blocking) on INDEX.md drift. Workflow-type aware (compressed, forwarded, standard). Per-slice review artifacts. Augmentations register on the workflow index and produce type-specific deliverables that downstream stages consume. v9.0.0 lands the four-router architecture; full release notes in plugins/sdlc-workflow/CHANGELOG.md.
